g.text: Signal Handler Execution Flow — Timer → Preemption
Key Insight: Split Between Interrupt and Switch
Signal handler runs in ~50ns. Does NOT switch contexts. Only sets flag. Coroutine state remains intact on its stack.
Actual context switch happens at safe point. Coroutine is at well-defined state. No partial register writes, no mid-operation corruption.
Signal handlers are severely restricted (async-signal-safe only). Cannot allocate, cannot lock mutexes, cannot safely switch contexts. Deferring the switch to safe point avoids all these dangers.
